name: Biscay Tour Trail: Gernika - Elantxobe Loop
name_original: Reserva de la Biosfera de Urdaibai y Elantxobe — circular desde Guernica
description: Cycle 20.5 miles (33.0 km) through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve from Guernica to Elantxobe, featuring moderate difficulty and coastal view
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Guernica y Luno traverse a region characterized by diverse landscapes, including coastal areas, the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, and rolling hills. The terrain offers a mix of mostly paved roads, with options ranging from moderate rides to challenging climbs with substantial elevation gains. Cyclists can explore routes that pass through significant natural features and historical landmarks within the Basque Country. Best road cycling routes around Guernica y Luno The most popular road cycling route is Biscay Tour Trail: Gernika - Elantxobe Loop, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Urdaibai estuary and coastal villages. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Monte Oiz and Balcón de Bizkaia Loop, a difficult 39.4 miles (63.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, ascending to Monte Oiz and offering panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the La Tejería de Murueta, Paresi, Garaioltza and return to Gernika — circular by the Río Oca, a 42.5 miles (68.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Guernica y Luno is defined by coastal paths, the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, and challenging hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to routes with significant climbs. The routes in Guernica y Luno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guernica y Luno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Guernica y Luno?
A: Guernica y Luno offers a diverse selection of over 20 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate rides to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking easier rides?
A: Yes, while many routes feature challenging climbs, there are options for less experienced cyclists. The region includes routes classified as moderate, providing a good balance for those looking for an enjoyable ride without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031485376/biscay-tour-trail-gernika-elantxobe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biscay Tour Trail: Gernika - Elantxobe Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.5-mile route offering views of the Urdaibai estuary.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists will find several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031503362/monte-oiz-and-balcon-de-bizkaia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oiz and Balcón de Bizkaia Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.4-mile path featuring substantial elevation gain to Monte Oiz, offering panoramic views. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032220163/la-tejeria-de-murueta-paresi-garaioltza-and-return-to-gernika-circular-by-the-rio-oca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tejería de Murueta, Paresi, Garaioltza and return to Gernika — circular by the Río Oca&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.5-mile trail with significant climbing.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Guernica y Luno?
A: Road cycling in Guernica y Luno offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter coastal areas, the stunning Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve with its estuary and marshlands, and rolling hills characteristic of the Basque Country. Many routes provide rewarding views of the sea and the Urdaibai estuary, such as those from Montecalvo.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes integrate cultural and natural attractions. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzubi Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6628930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oma Forest&lt;/a&gt; (Painted Forest). Many routes also offer breathtaking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Guernica y Luno?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for road cycling in Guernica y Luno. During these seasons, temperatures are pleasant, making for ideal riding conditions to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Guernica y Luno are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031503362/monte-oiz-and-balcon-de-bizkaia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oiz and Balcón de Bizkaia Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032220163/la-tejeria-de-murueta-paresi-garaioltza-and-return-to-gernika-circular-by-the-rio-oca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tejería de Murueta, Paresi, Garaioltza and return to Gernika — circular by the Río Oca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The region's proximity to the coast means many routes provide stunning coastal vistas. For instance, the 'Laga Beach – Coastal village loop from Gernika' offers expansive views of the coastline and destinations like Laga Beach within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What are the options for parking when starting a road cycling route?
A: While specific parking details for each route vary, Guernica y Luno, as a central hub, typically offers parking facilities. Many routes start and end near cultural landmarks in the town, where parking is generally available, allowing convenient access to your cycling adventure.

Q: Is public transport accessible for cyclists in Guernica y Luno?
A: Guernica y Luno is a well-connected town in the Basque Country. While specific provisions for bicycles on public transport can vary, it's advisable to check local train (Euskotren) or bus services for their bike policies if you plan to use them to reach your starting point.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many road cycling routes in Guernica y Luno feature significant elevation changes, some moderate routes or sections of longer routes might be suitable for families with older, more experienced children. For younger families, it's best to look for routes with minimal elevation gain and away from heavy traffic.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling experience in Guernica y Luno?
A: The road cycling routes in Guernica y Luno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area traverse the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area. This allows cyclists to experience its unique estuary, marshlands, and incredible landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031485376/biscay-tour-trail-gernika-elantxobe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biscay Tour Trail: Gernika - Elantxobe Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers views of the Urdaibai estuary.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in Guernica y Luno pass through or near villages and towns, providing opportunities to stop for refreshments. Guernica y Luno itself offers numerous cafes and restaurants, and smaller villages along routes like the Alto de Morga loop may also have local establishments.

Q: Are there routes that include cultural sites like the Guernica Mural or the Old Tree of Gernika?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to incorporate the rich cultural heritage of the region. Routes often start or end near significant historical sites in Guernica y Luno, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1923359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1050528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tree of Gernika (Gernikako Arbola)&lt;/a&gt;, allowing for a blend of physical activity and cultural exploration.
tips:
Its history of mills and ironworks becomes glamorous when Napoleon III builds a castle for his wife Eugenia de Montijo, next to the marsh. The castle is, together with the Urdaibai Bird Center, its tourist emblem, but due to its strategic location, with the marsh as the setting for a unique landscape, close to the historic town of Gernika-Lumo and a few kilometers from some of the most important beaches from Bizkaia, Gautegiz Arteaga has many interesting natural resources.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/gautegiz-arteaga/

Beautiful dune beach located in Ibarrangelu. With two different bathing areas, one with currents, facing the estuary, and the other open to the sea. Ideal for all types of public.
It has showers, toilets and changing rooms.
Mandatory stop, either to enjoy the views or to make a refreshment looking out onto the beach

Picturesque fishing village on the Basque coast, known for its unique location on a steep hillside that slopes down to the sea. The town, with old buildings and a beautiful church, offers impressive views of the Bay of Biscay.

Kortezubi is a municipality that preserves an extensive landscape, ecological and cultural heritage. It was chosen by our ancestors as a home and residence in prehistoric times, undoubtedly attracted by the abundance of resources and the comfort of its many caves. Proof of this is the Santimamiñe Cave. It enjoys a landscape open towards the estuary and secluded in the interior valleys, with walks and routes that immerse you in an exceptional landscape.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/kortezubi/

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. It requires good fitness due to its undulating terrain and an elevation gain of approximately 550 meters. While mostly well-paved, some segments might be more challenging.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, starting and ending in Guernica, taking you through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve and to Elantxobe before returning.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Guernica (Gernika). While specific parking details aren't provided, as a significant town, Guernica typically offers various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking facilities upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved surfaces, with about 32.1 km of asphalt and 867m of paved sections. The terrain is undulating, featuring gentle mountains and hills that gradually slope towards the coast, offering a varied cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and notable stops. You'll pass by the impressive Arteaga Castle, enjoy views of Laida Beach, and get a View of Laga Beach. The charming fishing village of Elantxobe is a highlight, known for its houses clinging to a steep hillside. You'll also be near the Santimamiñe Cave Paintings in Kortezubi.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve benefits from an oceanic climate, making it green and vibrant year-round. However, for road cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather conditions. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer days.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling path. While dogs are generally welcome in outdoor areas, it's important to consider their safety and comfort on a road cycling route. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are secured in a suitable trailer or carrier designed for cycling, as riding alongside a bike on roads can be dangerous for them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve is a protected natural area, but there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the reserve on public roads. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles to preserve its ecological wealth.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Guernica at the start/end and the village of Elantxobe, a key point on the route, offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel and rest. You may also find options in other smaller villages you pass through.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. Notably, it covers 51% of the Bizkaiko bira and 12% of the Laida-Laga trail. Smaller portions also intersect with paths like Atxarre. Artadiaren bihotzaren erdian and Gernika-Lumo Forua. Estuarioaren bihotzean barrena.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular and diverse landscapes, including rolling green hills, lush forests, and impressive cliffs meeting the Cantabrian coast. Expect charming villages, views of the sea and the Mundaka estuary, and the unique biodiversity of its marshes. The route also offers panoramic vistas from various viewpoints.
